title: Medication adherence hypertension review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

medication.text: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.
medication.rationale: CG76 directly supports non-judgemental documentation of recent medicine-taking behaviour, missed doses, intentional or practical barriers, and preferred support without changing treatment.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

source.title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support

medication.text: Supports clinic, home, or ambulatory blood-pressure monitoring, symptoms, medication and lifestyle discussion, and an annual review without generating targets, treatment, or advice.
medication.rationale: NG136 supports documenting already measured clinic or home blood pressure, symptoms, annual-review context, and clinician-entered follow-up without inferring control or changing medicines.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinic, home, or ambulatory blood-pressure monitoring, symptoms, medication and lifestyle discussion, and an annual review without generating targets, treatment, or advice.

source.title: Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management

medication.text: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.
medication.rationale: Beliefs and concerns were reviewed for adherence applicability and remain clinician-entered.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

source.title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.19–1.1.23 — knowledge, beliefs, and concerns about medicines

source_limitations: Medicine names, indications, doses, schedule, actual adherence, reasons, adverse effects, blood-pressure values, home technique, comorbidities, interactions, clinician interpretation, changes, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
